context_only: # tp v0.26.0 — Presence-preserving task-file workflow (no default materialization)

tasks.title: Add commit_strategy to WorkflowOverride
tasks.acceptance: WorkflowOverride gains a CommitStrategy *string field (model/projectconfig.go); parseWorkflowOverride reads the commit_strategy key into it; ResolveWorkflowLayers resolves commit_strategy via pickString. A unit test covers parse and resolution of commit_strategy.
tasks.closed_reason:
- WorkflowOverride gained a CommitStrategy *string with a commit_strategy,omitempty json tag (internal/model/projectconfig.go)
- parseWorkflowOverride reads the commit_strategy key into wo.CommitStrategy and knownWorkflowKeys accepts it (internal/engine/projectconfig.go)
- ResolveWorkflowLayers resolves commit_strategy via pickString over the task and project layers (internal/engine/configresolve.go)
- TestResolveWorkflowLayers_CommitStrategy covers parse and resolution precedence (internal/engine/configresolve_test.go)

tasks.title: Store task-file workflow as presence-tracked override
tasks.acceptance:
TaskFile.Workflow is stored as the presence-tracked WorkflowOverride; the storage path applies no built-in defaults on unmarshal and every effective-value read goes through the resolver (EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile), enforced by the type change.
WriteTaskFile round-trips presence: a "workflow": {} block stays {}, a {"review_max_rounds":4} block keeps exactly that field, the workflow key is always emitted as an object, and a source with no workflow key normalizes to {} (tests for the round-trip matrix, quality_gate and commit_strategy).
No read-modify-write materializes a default field.

tasks.title: Clamp out-of-range task override at resolution
tasks.acceptance:
The resolution path clamps out-of-range task-override values to absent (clampWorkflowRanges applied to the task override) so the value resolves through the precedence (project value if set, else built-in default) and never masks a lower layer.
A warning is emitted to stderr and the command exits 0; an acceptance test covers no-project-config (resolves to 2) and project-config-present (review_clean_rounds:3 resolves to 3).
tasks.closed_reason:
- The resolution path clamps out-of-range task overrides to absent via clampWorkflowRanges in ResolveEffectiveWorkflow/EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (internal/engine/configresolve.go); TestResolveEffectiveWorkflow_OutOfRangeTaskOverrideClamped asserts review_clean_rounds:0 resolves to the built-in default 2 with an "out of range" warning (internal/engine/clamp_resolve_test.go)
- TestResolveEffectiveWorkflow_OutOfRangeDoesNotMaskProject asserts an out-of-range override resolves through the project value 3 rather than masking it to the built-in, and warnings surface to stderr while tp config exits 0 (internal/cli/config.go)

tasks.title: tp init writes a sparse workflow block
tasks.acceptance: tp init with no workflow flags writes "workflow": {}; --quality-gate writes exactly {"quality_gate": ...}; --commit-strategy writes exactly {"commit_strategy": ...}; both flags compose to {"quality_gate": ..., "commit_strategy": ...}; no convergence/timeout/checks field is written unless a flag sets it. An acceptance test covers all four cases.
tasks.closed_reason:
- No-flag tp init writes "workflow": {} — init.go constructs model.WorkflowOverride{} with no materialized fields (internal/cli/init.go); TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock/no_flags_writes_empty_object
- --quality-gate writes exactly {"quality_gate": <cmd>} — init.go sets only tf.Workflow.QualityGate; TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock/quality_gate_only
- --commit-strategy writes exactly {"commit_strategy": <value>} — init.go sets only tf.Workflow.CommitStrategy; TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock/commit_strategy_only
- Both flags compose to {"quality_gate", "commit_strategy"} — the two independent ifs set both pointers; TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock/both_flags_compose
- No convergence/timeout/checks field is written unless a flag sets it — the WorkflowOverride pointers stay nil and are omitted from JSON (internal/cli/init.go)
- TestInit_SparseWorkflowBlock (internal/cli/init_sparse_test.go) covers all four §10.1 cases with exact-equality assertions on the workflow block

tasks.title: tp set --workflow writes override fields only
tasks.acceptance:
tp set --workflow sets only the named override pointer field (no sibling materialized); the settable set is the five convergence/timeout integers plus checks.
Validation happens before write: an out-of-range or non-parseable value exits 1; an unknown or non-settable field name (including quality_gate and commit_strategy) exits 2; the task file is left unmodified on any rejection. Acceptance tests cover a successful set, an out-of-range reject, and a non-settable reject.
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp set --workflow writes only the named override pointer field with no materialized siblings (internal/cli/set.go); TestSetWorkflow.../successful_set asserts the block equals exactly {review_max_rounds: 4}
- The settable set is the five convergence/timeout integers plus checks (editableWorkflowFields); quality_gate and commit_strategy are in readOnlyWorkflowFields, authored only by tp init (internal/cli/set.go)
- An out-of-range value exits 1 and a non-parseable value exits 1 via strconv.Atoi + ExitValidation (internal/cli/set.go); the out-of-range and non-parseable subtests assert exit 1
- An unknown or non-settable field name (including quality_gate/commit_strategy) exits 2 via ExitUsage; the non-settable and unknown subtests assert exit 2
- Every rejected set leaves the task file unmodified because validation precedes the WithFileLock write; each reject subtest asserts the workflow block stays empty
- TestSetWorkflow_ErrorTaxonomyAndPresence (internal/cli/set_workflow_presence_test.go) covers successful set, out-of-range, non-parseable, non-settable, and unknown cases

tasks.title: config --resolved source for clamped fields
tasks.acceptance: tp config --resolved reports source "override" only for an in-range task-set field; a present-but-out-of-range field reports the resolved value with source "project" or "default", never "override". An acceptance test covers the no-masking case and the clamped-source case.
tasks.closed_reason:
- resolveConfigWorkflow clamps the override via engine.ClampWorkflowRanges before source attribution, so a present-but-out-of-range field is dropped to absent (internal/cli/config.go, internal/engine/projectconfig.go)
- tp config --resolved reports source "override" only for an in-range task-set field; TestResolvedConfig_SourceLabels covers override/project/default for in-range fields (internal/cli/config_test.go)
- A clamped out-of-range field reports the resolved value with source "project"/"default", never "override"; TestResolvedConfig_ClampedFieldReportsResolvedSource asserts review_clean_rounds:0 over project 3 reports {value:3, source:project} (internal/cli/config_clamp_test.go)

tasks.title: Thin spec/0.25.0.tasks.json workflow block
tasks.acceptance: spec/0.25.0.tasks.json workflow block is thinned to "workflow": {} only after tp config --resolved reports an identical value for every workflow field before and after thinning; the thinned file is committed.
